image_caption: Nhāykã Bahī, inner entrance, view from W
monument_description: Nhāykã Bahī is one of 21 prominent bahīs in Patan.
door_peculiarities: There is 1 carved door which serves as a main entry. On either side of the doorway are 2 blind windows (on the eastern facade of the ground floor), and 2 square lattice windows (on the first floor). The carved door is on the west-wing ground floor of the shrine. The door is intricately carved with images of wisdom bearers in the lintel end and a Buddha image at the center of the door frame, alongside two other images. The blind windows bear the image of the shrine's deity on the vāhana with other images.

image_caption: Yatkhā Bāhāḥ, fragment of a private house with a five-bay window, view from N
monument_description: Yatkhā Bāhāḥ, also known as Bhāṣkarakīrti Vihāra, is located in a rectangular residential courtyard belonging to families of the Tāmrākāra caste group.
door_peculiarities:
An elaborately carved five-bay window adornes the first floor and is flanked by two small single openings.

The second floor features a large five-bayed balcony window which incoorporates six brackets dating from the thirteenth century. It is also flanked by two simple single windows.

In addition, there are two blind windows on the ground floor and two blind windows on the second floor.

image_caption: Nhāykã Bahī, inner entrance, view from W
monument_description: Nhāykã Bahī is one of 21 prominent bahīs in Patan.
door_peculiarities: There is 1 carved door which serves as a main entry. On either side of the doorway are 2 blind windows (on the eastern facade of the ground floor), and 2 square lattice windows (on the first floor). The carved door is on the west-wing ground floor of the shrine. The door is intricately carved with images of wisdom bearers in the lintel end and a Buddha image at the center of the door frame, alongside two other images. The blind windows bear the image of the shrine's deity on the vāhana with other images.

image_caption: Yatkhā Bāhāḥ, fragment of a private house with a five-bay window, view from N
monument_description: Yatkhā Bāhāḥ, also known as Bhāṣkarakīrti Vihāra, is located in a rectangular residential courtyard belonging to families of the Tāmrākāra caste group.
door_peculiarities:
An elaborately carved five-bay window adornes the first floor and is flanked by two small single openings.

The second floor features a large five-bayed balcony window which incoorporates six brackets dating from the thirteenth century. It is also flanked by two simple single windows.

In addition, there are two blind windows on the ground floor and two blind windows on the second floor.

image_caption: Iku Bāhāḥ, view from N (photo by Bruce Owens, 2018)
monument_description: Iku Bāhāḥ is one of the branches of Bu Bāhāḥ ( LAL0440 ) and is located to the north-west of the main bāhāḥ .
door_peculiarities:
Two historic latticed windows on the ground floor remain. The metal-clad main door and toraṇa above likely date from the mid 19th century. The carved wooden door might be older.

The third floor features one modern window frame. The structure is under construction (March 2019) and temporarily covered with corrugated metal sheets.

image_caption: Cilancva Bāhāḥ, view from NW
monument_description: The Cilancva area is the highest point in Kirtipur, located in its eastern part.
door_peculiarities:
The monument has three wooden carved doors on the façade of its ground floor. The central door constitutes the main entrance to the sanctum which is heavily carved in comparision to the other two doors. This central door is also flanked by two small carved windows.

The first floor of the monument has five windows. Three of them are pasukājhyāḥs. The central pasukājhyāḥ is five-bayed whereas the other two are three-bayed. The central five-bayed pasukājhyāḥ is also flanked by two smaller carved windows.

image_caption: Buṅga Bah, main entrance, view from E; padlock ( bhoṭe tālcā ) on the door, view from S; 2019-10-13
monument_description: Buṅga Bahī, also known as Prathampura Mahāvihāra, is located at the northwestern end of Buṅgamatī.
door_peculiarities: The front door of the main sanctum on the ground floor is carved with fine latticed door leaves. The door is flanked by two vertical panels (measuring 155 x 22 cm) that each feature a ghandarva with a human upper body and the hindquarters of a bird. This creature is holding the vase of plenty in both hands alongside a female figure with crossed legs holding a scarf ( see Gutschow 2011, 223).

image_caption: Dune Bāhāḥ, Gaṇeśa statue, view from E
monument_description: Dune Bāhāḥ, one of the branches of Oku Bāhāḥ ( LAL2260 ), is located in Okubāhāl Ṭola to the north of Oku Bāhāḥ.
door_peculiarities: The doors and windows have some peculiarities. The main central door is 165cm high, and is decorated with floral patterns on its architraves. The head of the door frame contains three images of Buddha. An image of a deity can be found on either side of the lintel ends. Two small windows with vertical rails flank the main door. The first floor central window is a pasukājhyāḥ with three openings flanked by two small arched openings.

The first floor central window is a pasukājhyāḥ with three openings flanked by two small arched openings.

image_caption: Ibāḥ Bahī, main door to the sanctum, view from E
monument_description: Ibāḥ Bahī is one of the major monasteries of Patan.
door_peculiarities: The main door is highly decorated with images of wisdom bearers on the lintel ends. The lintel ends are also covered in carvings of of foliage and lotus scrolls. The main jambs of the door frame display images of deities at the bottom and have been fixed on a timber threshold. Above the door frame, there is an image of Buddha flanked by two maids holding cāmara.

door_peculiarities: The main door on the ground floor to the east is well carved and painted. A peculiar feature can be seen above the stone threshold; the usual carving of Bhairava is replaced by carvings of the newly born Buddha and his parents Suddhodana and Mayādevī.
